description: In Albanian, the rhotic /r/ exhibits allophonic variation: alveolar flap [ɾ] in most positions, retroflex flap [ɽ] in syllable codas, and alveolar approximant [ɹ] at word-final positions. This variation is systematic and reflects positional allophony in Albanian phonology.
sources: Belluscio, A. (2014). _Rhotic Allophony in Northern Tosk Albanian_. In G. Gesuato & F. Grazia Busà (Eds.), _Proceedings of the 5th Conference on Laboratory Phonology_.
Cambridge University Press. (2022). _Northern Tosk Albanian_. Journal of the International Phonetic Association.
Hysenaj, B. (2009). _Phonological Variation in Albanian Dialects_. Tirana: Academy of Sciences.
